name: Light Pumpernickel
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to the temperature specified in your recipe.
2. Place the yeast in a bowl and sprinkle with water. Allow it to sit for about 5 minutes until foamy.
3. In a large mixing bowl, combine the water, vegetable oil, molasses, gluten (if using), sugar, salt, caraway seeds, unsweetened cocoa, rye flour, bread flour, and yeast.
4. Mix all ingredients together until a dough forms.
5. Turn the dough out onto a floured surface and knead for about 10 minutes until smooth and elastic.
6. Place the dough in a greased bowl, cover with a damp cloth, and let it rise in a warm place for about 1 hour or until doubled in size.
7. Preheat oven to the temperature specified in your recipe (usually around 400°F/200°C).
8. Shape the risen dough into a loaf and place on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
9. Bake for the time specified in your recipe, usually around 30-40 minutes, until golden brown and crusty.
10. Turn off the oven when ready and let cool before slicing and serving.
